req.title: Data retention (boundary)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers nightly retention job behaviour on /cron/retention. Three orthogonal scenarios are needed: (1) EP positive – a clearly expired resource is purged and audit log written; (2) NEG negative – a policy mismatch triggers an incident rather than silent purge; (3) BVA boundary – a resource whose expiry timestamp is exactly midnight of the current day (the purge boundary) is handled correctly. Each test seeds its own state, triggers the cron endpoint, and asserts outcomes without shared mutable state, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_expired_resource_purged_and_audit_written
preconditions: A resource with expiry date 30 days in the past exists in the datastore
actions: Seed a resource with expiry = today - 30 days via <seed_resource_fixture>
POST /cron/retention to trigger the nightly job
GET the seeded resource to confirm deletion
GET /audit/log and filter for the resource ID
expected: GET of deleted resource returns HTTP 404
Audit log contains an entry with action=PURGE and resource_id matching the seeded record
trace: R-HLT-045: expired resources shall be purged
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_expired_resource_purged_and_audit_written():
    expiry = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_date=expiry)

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{resource_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404

    audit_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/audit/log", params={"resource_id": resource_id})
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entries", [])
    purge_entries = [e for e in entries if e.get("action") == "PURGE"]
    assert len(purge_entries) >= 1
    assert purge_entries[0]["resource_id"] == resource_id

tests.name: test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident
preconditions: A resource exists whose category has no matching retention policy configured
actions: Seed a resource with category=UNKNOWN_POLICY via <seed_resource_fixture>
POST /cron/retention to trigger the nightly job
GET /incidents and filter for the resource ID
expected: POST /cron/retention returns HTTP 200 (job completes; incident raised internally)
An incident record exists referencing the resource_id with reason=POLICY_MISMATCH
The resource itself is NOT purged (remains retrievable)
trace: R-HLT-045: incident raised on policy mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident():
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(
        expiry_date=None,
        category="UNKNOWN_POLICY"
    )

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    incident_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/incidents",
        params={"resource_id": resource_id}
    )
    assert incident_resp.status_code == 200
    incidents = incident_resp.json().get("incidents", [])
    mismatch = [i for i in incidents if i.get("reason") == "POLICY_MISMATCH"]
    assert len(mismatch) >= 1

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{resource_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Resource must NOT be purged on policy mismatch"

tests.name: test_resource_expiring_exactly_at_midnight_boundary
preconditions: Retention policy purges resources whose expiry timestamp <= start-of-today (00:00:00 UTC)
Two resources seeded: one expiring at 23:59:59 yesterday (must purge), one at 00:00:00 today (boundary – must purge)
actions: Seed resource_A with expiry_ts = today 00:00:00 UTC exactly via <seed_resource_fixture>
Seed resource_B with expiry_ts = today 00:00:01 UTC (one second after boundary) via <seed_resource_fixture>
GET resource_A and resource_B to check presence
expected: resource_A (expiry == midnight) is purged – GET returns 404
resource_B (expiry one second after midnight) is retained – GET returns 200
Audit log contains PURGE entry for resource_A only
trace: R-HLT-045: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
R-HLT-045: expired resources shall be purged
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_resource_expiring_exactly_at_midnight_boundary():
    today_midnight = datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(
        hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0
    )
    one_second_after = today_midnight.replace(second=1)

    id_a = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_ts=today_midnight.isoformat())
    id_b = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_ts=one_second_after.isoformat())

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    assert requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{id_a}").status_code == 404
    assert requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{id_b}").status_code == 200

    audit = requests.get(f"{BASE}/audit/log", params={"resource_id": id_a}).json()
    purged = [e for e in audit.get("entries", []) if e.get("action") == "PURGE"]
    assert len(purged) >= 1

    audit_b = requests.get(f"{BASE}/audit/log", params={"resource_id": id_b}).json()
    purged_b = [e for e in audit_b.get("entries", []) if e.get("action") == "PURGE"]
    assert len(purged_b) == 0
